name: North Korea IRBM Test with Maneuverable Reentry Vehicle
target: East Sea / Sea of Japan splash zone
description: North Korea launched a solid-fuel IRBM fitted with a maneuverable reentry vehicle from the Sunan area near Pyongyang, traveling approximately 1,000 km.

name: Pulhwasal-3-31 Submarine-Launched Cruise Missile Tests
description: North Korea conducted two test launches of the Pulhwasal-3-31 submarine-launched strategic cruise missile from a submarine off Sinpo Naval Base.

name: Freedom Shield 2024 Exercise
description: The US and ROK conducted the annual 11-day Freedom Shield exercise, for the first time rehearsing responses to a hypothetical North Korean nuclear strike.

name: Hwasong-16B Hypersonic IRBM Test
target: East Sea target at 1,000 km range
description: North Korea test-launched the Hwasong-16B, its first solid-fueled IRBM equipped with a hypersonic glide vehicle, reaching peaks of 101.1 km and 72.3 km altitude.

name: Failed Malligyong-1-1 Spy Satellite Launch
description: A new-type rocket carrying the Malligyong-1-1 spy satellite launched from Sohae but exploded shortly after liftoff. North Korea's fourth satellite attempt after one success in November 2023.

name: North Korea Trash Balloon Campaign Begins
target: South Korean territory south of DMZ
description: North Korea launched 260 balloons carrying feces, garbage, and waste batteries across the DMZ. Over the summer, approximately 2,000 trash-filled balloons were sent as retaliation for anti-DPRK leaflets.

name: South Korea Resumes DMZ Loudspeaker Broadcasts
description: South Korea redeployed loudspeakers along the DMZ for the first time in six years, broadcasting anti-DPRK propaganda and K-pop in response to the trash balloon campaign.

name: Inaugural Freedom Edge Trilateral Exercise
description: The US, ROK, and Japan conducted the first-ever Freedom Edge exercise involving the USS Theodore Roosevelt carrier strike group, creating a new framework for trilateral security cooperation.

name: North Korea GPS Spoofing Campaign
target: South Korean civilian aircraft and maritime vessels
description: North Korea conducted GPS spoofing operations near the border, disrupting navigation for 201 aircraft and 731 ships by feeding false position data to approaching aircraft.

name: North Korea Enacts Updated Nuclear Doctrine Law
description: Kim Jong Un declared DPRK's nuclear status 'irreversible,' authorizing preemptive nuclear strikes and mandating automatic launch if leadership command-and-control is threatened.

name: North Korea Demolishes Inter-Korean Roads and Railways
target: Gyeongui and Donghae inter-Korean road and rail links
description: The KPA detonated 60-meter stretches of both inter-Korean roads and rail lines just 10 meters north of the MDL, symbolizing the declaration that North and South are 'two hostile countries at war.'

name: DPRK Troops Deploy to Russian Kursk Front
description: Approximately 10,000-12,000 North Korean soldiers deployed to Russia's Kursk Oblast to fight against Ukrainian forces. NATO confirmed the deployment on October 28.

name: Hwasong-19 Solid-Fuel ICBM First Test
target: Sea of Japan west of Okushiri Island
description: The Hwasong-19 flew 85.93 minutes to 7,687.5 km altitude — the longest flight for any DPRK ICBM. Likely intended for MIRV capability.

name: South Korea Martial Law Crisis and Presidential Impeachment
description: President Yoon declared martial law at 22:27, but 190 legislators unanimously lifted it by 01:02. Yoon was impeached December 14 and removed April 4, 2025 — the most severe political disruption since democratization.

name: Lazarus Group Bybit $1.5 Billion Cryptocurrency Theft
description: North Korea's Lazarus Group executed the largest cryptocurrency theft in history via a supply chain compromise. DPRK has stolen over $5 billion in crypto since 2017.

name: Freedom Shield 2025 Exercise
description: Annual exercise tested upgraded responses to DPRK nuclear threats including drones, GPS jamming, and cyberattack scenarios amid South Korea's political instability.

name: DPRK 'Nuclear Trigger' Tactical Nuclear Strike Drill
description: Kim Jong Un oversaw a 'joint striking drill' using the national combined nuclear weapons management system, launching KN-25 rockets and at least one SRBM from Wonsan.

name: Hwasong-20 ICBM Unveiled at WPK 80th Anniversary Parade
description: North Korea unveiled the Hwasong-20, described as its 'strongest nuclear strategic weapon,' on an unprecedented 11-axle TEL. China's Premier Li Qiang and Russia's Medvedev attended.

name: Hwasong-11E Hypersonic SRBM Dual Launch
target: Tableland target in North Hamgyong Province
description: Two hypersonic SRBMs traveled 350 km northeast, striking a tableland target. US Indo-Pacific Command condemned the tests ahead of the APEC summit.

name: Long-Range Strategic Cruise Missile Test
description: North Korea's final weapons test of 2025, capping a year of significant missile development including the Hwasong-20 unveiling, multiple hypersonic tests, and the Nuclear Trigger system debut.
